title: oenothera macrocarpa var. incana
intro: Oenothera macrocarpa var. incana, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Onagraceae family. The plant is valued for its seeds, which are rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid with medicinal properties. The GLA in Oenothera macrocarpa var. incana has been used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, and to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a dietary supplement, oil, or herbal tea, with capsules or softgels being common forms of herbal preparation.
